scenario_name: Customer-facing RAG chatbot over retail-banking knowledge base
description: A retail bank deploys a customer-facing chatbot that answers product, fee, and account-handling questions by retrieving from a curated knowledge corpus (policy documents, FAQ articles, product disclosures). The chatbot is fronted by the bank's authenticated web and mobile channels; authenticated sessions may also query a limited tool that resolves the customer's own account balance and recent transactions. The deployment uses a hosted third-party LLM, an in-region managed vector store, an internally-maintained RAG ingestion pipeline, and a guardrail layer fronting both prompts and outputs. This archetype is the canonical example for the dataset.
assumptions: All KB content is owned by the bank; no third-party ingestion at runtime.
Customer account-balance tool is read-only and scoped to the authenticated customer.
LLM provider is contracted under a BAA-equivalent enterprise agreement with no training on customer data.

components.description: Hosted third-party general-purpose LLM (enterprise tier, customer-data-isolation contract).
Provider-hosted embedding model used for KB chunking and query encoding.
Managed in-region vector store with per-collection ACLs.
Internal batch ingestion service pulling KB articles from a CMS, chunking, embedding, and indexing.
Account-balance lookup tool, read-only, scoped to authenticated customer's own accounts.
Input + output safety classifiers covering PII regex detection, jailbreak signatures, and toxic content.
Bank IDP issuing short-lived tokens that bind chatbot turns to the authenticated customer identity.
Centralized logging of prompts and responses with field-level redaction and 30-day retention.
Web and mobile chat front-ends inside the authenticated banking portal.

risks_identified.rationale: RAG retrieves policy documents that may contain customer-specific examples and the account-balance tool returns sensitive financial data into the model context.
risks_identified.residual_rationale: Retrieval is scoped per-customer; output guardrails redact PII; logs are field-level redacted.

risks_identified.rationale: Account-balance tool credentials must be scoped per-session; misconfiguration enables cross-customer data access.
risks_identified.residual_rationale: Tool uses short-lived per-session tokens from the IDP; calls are end-to-end audited.

risks_identified.rationale: Indirect prompt injection via KB content could coerce the agent to misuse the account-balance tool.
risks_identified.residual_rationale: KB ingestion has an editorial review gate; injection-detection runs at retrieval time.

risks_identified.rationale: GDPR, CCPA, GLBA, and (for EU footprint) DORA + EU AI Act obligations apply to customer chat content.
risks_identified.residual_rationale: DPIA completed; contractual processor terms in place; consumer rights handling integrated.

risks_identified.rationale: Shared vector store and shared LLM session-cache create cross-customer leakage exposure if isolation breaks.
risks_identified.residual_rationale: Per-customer retrieval filter on every query; no cross-session memory store.

risks_identified.rationale: Account-balance tool is constrained read-only but any future write tool would expose the LLM-to-DB gateway risk.
risks_identified.residual_rationale: Current scope read-only; write expansion requires explicit security review.

risks_identified.rationale: Vector store contains chunked KB content and potentially memorized customer-data fragments from prior ingestion runs.
risks_identified.residual_rationale: Vector store under same KMS as primary KB; tenant isolation by collection.

risks_identified.rationale: Logged prompts and responses can contain sensitive financial data and PII.
risks_identified.residual_rationale: Logs are field-level redacted at ingestion; 30-day retention.

risks_identified.rationale: Over-broad context could include policy material above the customer's need-to-know.
risks_identified.residual_rationale: Context truncation policy in place but not topic-aware.

risks_identified.rationale: Customer-facing surface is a regulated availability concern; LLM-provider outage propagates to channel impact.
risks_identified.residual_rationale: Static fallback flow is implemented; multi-provider failover not yet.

risks_identified.rationale: Adversarial submissions through public-facing surfaces (complaints, social media monitored content) could be indexed into the KB if processes weaken.
risks_identified.residual_rationale: KB ingestion strictly from CMS-authored content; no external feed at runtime.

existing_controls.description: Per-customer retrieval scope: every retrieval query is bound to the authenticated customer's tenant filter.
Short-lived per-session tokens from the IDP for the account-balance tool; no static service credentials.
Input and output PII regex classifiers with allow/deny lists tuned to banking domain (account numbers, SSN, card PAN).
Editorial review gate on KB ingestion; injection-detection at retrieval time on retrieved chunks.
Field-level redaction at log ingestion; 30-day retention with KMS-managed access controls.
DPIA completed; consumer-rights handling pipeline integrated with the bank's DSAR process.
Markdown rendering in chat UI is sanitized; external image hosts are blocklisted from the assistant's allowed-egress set.
Static fallback flow when the LLM provider is degraded; routes customers to scripted responses or human agents.

control_gaps.description: No multi-provider LLM failover; outages propagate to channel availability.
control_gaps.suggested_remediation: Add a second LLM provider configured as warm standby; periodically exercise failover.

control_gaps.description: Context truncation is not topic-aware; over-broad context may be assembled for unrelated questions.
control_gaps.suggested_remediation: Implement task-classifier-driven context windows that retrieve only the chunks relevant to the predicted task class.

control_gaps.description: Embedding inversion risk on the vector store is not explicitly tested.
control_gaps.suggested_remediation: Run an annual Vec2Text-style inversion test on a representative sample; rotate embedding model if reconstruction exceeds threshold.

control_gaps.description: Adversarial-input red-team exercise not yet a release gate.
control_gaps.suggested_remediation: Add a published red-team corpus to the release gate; track refusal stability across versions.

framework_alignments.notes: KMS-managed encryption across KB, vector store, logs.
Short-lived IDP tokens; no static service credentials.
Prompt/response monitoring exists; injection-rate anomaly detection not yet.

framework_alignments.control_name: AI system security is evaluated and documented
framework_alignments.notes: Red-team exercises run; not yet a release gate.

framework_alignments.control_name: Policies and procedures are in place for AI risk management
framework_alignments.notes: DPIA + AI risk policy maintained by the bank's model risk function.
KB and embedding store have classification labels and provenance.
Detection in place; adversarial release-gate testing planned.
PII regex on input and output; logs redacted.
DPIA on file with the bank's DPO.

framework_alignments.control_name: Obligations of deployers of high-risk AI systems

framework_alignments.notes: Inherited from underlying SOC 2 Type II program.

notes: This entry is the canonical example for the dataset. It assumes a relatively mature financial-services deployment; less mature organizations should expect more 'partial' or 'missing' framework alignments and more 'Immediate'-urgency gaps. The archetype deliberately omits trading-floor and credit-decisioning use cases, which carry different risk profiles.
